title: Jaylen Brown opens up about past criticisms of his left hand: ‘There was some truth to it’
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jaylen Brown acknowledges past struggles with his left hand due to a serious wrist injury and surgeries in 2021 and 2023.
Improving his left-hand play, Brown uses stem cells for wrist recovery, enhancing his game with increased flexibility and motion.
A standout season sees Brown achieving career-highs: 28.7 points, 7.0 rebounds, 5.3 assists, positioning him in MVP talks.

title: ‘The Red Sox were my dream job’: Don Orsillo reflects on NESN tenure, reunites with former colleagues
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Don Orsillo, former NESN Red Sox announcer, reflected on his tenure during a visit to Boston with the San Diego Padres.
Orsillo worked as the Red Sox play-by-play voice from 2001 to 2015 and is now with the Padres since 2016.
During his return, Orsillo reunited with former colleagues Jim Rice and Tom Caron, sharing moments on social media.

title: Alex Cora explains his decision to slot Roman Anthony at DH early this season
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora used Roman Anthony as DH due to defensive struggles early this season.
Anthony contributed offensively with a triple and a base hit against the Padres, but struggled defensively.
Cora defended Anthony after an error, praising improvement in his throwing angle and power despite a costly play.

title: Connelly Early gave Red Sox a chance to win despite high pitch count, lack of command
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Connelly Early held the Padres to two runs through four innings despite walking four batters and struggling with command.
Boston Red Sox lost to the San Diego Padres 3-2, with the deciding run scored in the ninth inning off Aroldis Chapman.
Early's six career MLB starts have yielded 39 strikeouts, tying Daisuke Matsuzaka for most in franchise history over first six games.

title: 4 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-0 win over CF Montreal
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution defeated CF Montreal 3-0, with goals from Luca Langoni, Mamadou Fofana, and Peyton Miller.
Revolution head coach Marko Mitrović received a red card for leaving the technical area during the 34th minute.
Mamadou Fofana scored from a set piece, celebrating with set piece coach Marc Ortí Esteban on the sidelines.

title: Here’s the latest on Red Sox’ Triston Casas, Johan Oviedo injuries
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox's Triston Casas set to resume hitting activities after rib injury; experienced soreness from late March swings.
Johan Oviedo faces elbow strain; will consult Tommy John surgeon as velocity dip raises concerns.
Kutter Crawford and Patrick Sandoval nearing minor league rehab assignments after lengthy injury recoveries.

title: This 22-year-old cancer survivor is running Boston for MGH
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Grace Taylor, a 22-year-old cancer survivor, will run the Boston Marathon for Mass General Hospital.
Taylor was diagnosed with a rare thyroid cancer at 19 and treated at Mass General Hospital.
She received support from MGH's AYA program and credits it for her recovery and marathon participation.

title: South Boston runner supports BMC social workers in Boston Marathon
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Paige Beattie, 23, from South Boston, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon with Team BMC.
Beattie seeks to raise $10,000 for Boston Medical Center's safety-net mission and its social workers.
She highlights the vital role of social workers in supporting patients, inspired by personal connections.

title: Patience remains an elusive virtue for Legacy FC attack
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC lost 1-0 to San Diego Wave at Gillette Stadium, remaining winless in NWSL's regular season.
Coach Filipa Patão cited rush and lack of patience as key issues in Boston's attacking third against San Diego.
Forward Amanda Gutierres struggled with passing accuracy, completing 11 of 21 passes, and remains goalless in four appearances.

title: Melrose man charged with stealing hundreds of thousands from clients, prosecutors say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Henry Rappa Jr., 57, charged with embezzling client funds and unauthorized law practice despite suspension.
Rappa allegedly misused approximately $660,000 from a client, delaying return despite requests.
Rappa handled real estate closing post-suspension, failed to distribute proceeds, retaining $21,300.
